title: Falling Edge Detector
description: Emits a single 1-tick pulse when a signal turns OFF (falling edge). Built with a NOT gate and a repeater creating a brief overlap: when input goes low the NOT torch lights immediately, but the repeater-delayed copy of the old high signal briefly suppresses it — net result is one pulse on the falling edge.

steps.title: Foundation
steps.explanation: 4-wide, 1-deep stone base. Signal enters from x=0, NOT torch is at x=1, repeater provides the delayed copy at x=2.

steps.title: Input lever
steps.explanation: Lever at (0,1,0). Dust at (0,1,0) fans both east to the repeater and into the NOT branch. Keep it ON to set up — the pulse fires when you turn it OFF.

steps.title: NOT branch + repeater delay
steps.explanation: Dust at (0,1,0) powers block at (1,0,0), so its east-face torch (NOT gate at (1,0,0) facing east) is OFF while lever is ON. A repeater at (1,1,0) facing east with delay=1 also passes the raw signal to (2,1,0).

steps.title: Output lamp
steps.explanation: Dust at (3,1,0) — connected to NOT torch output and the repeater output. Normally the repeater keeps (3,1,0) powered so the NOT torch can't fire the lamp. When lever turns OFF: NOT lights immediately, repeater still holds 1 tick, creating a 1-tick pulse at (3,1,0) before the repeater drops. Lamp at (3,0,-1) flashes once.
